finding: A model weighting accumulated outcomes against adjusted delays explains choices between multi-outcome sequences and predicts violations of independence and preference reversals across conditions.

Sexual minority status modifies the developmental trajectory of body-related concerns; gay and bisexual individuals show greater increases in toned-appearance concerns and weight-shape concerns with age compared to heterosexual peers.
Incidental cues to an economic concept increase endorsement of system-justifying beliefs, including acceptance of inequality and beliefs that disadvantaged groups deserve their status.
Overconfidence in relative ability enhances social status and increases in a group setting; a status-seeking motive promotes overconfident beliefs.
Different featural properties of objects are forgotten independently over time rather than as a bound unit; memory correlation between features decreases after longer delays.
Negative treatment is reciprocated more readily than positive treatment when payback to a different recipient is possible; this asymmetry is driven by negative affect.

Learning through active selection of observations outperforms passive reception of the same observations; this advantage stems from hypothesis-dependent sampling bias in self-directed data collection.
Unconscious deliberation improves deception detection accuracy above chance, outperforming conscious deliberation by allowing integration of rich information cues.
Reminders of structure in unrelated domains increase willingness to pursue goal-directed action.
Articulation of words with inward-directed mouth movements (resembling swallowing) promotes approach-like preferences; outward movements promote avoidance-like preferences.

Unemployment causes significant shifts in mean personality levels on agreeableness, conscientiousness, and openness; reemployment produces limited personality change, consistent with context-dependent personality malleability.
Observers simultaneously represent actions of multiple agents in the motor system; identical actions produce additive imitation effects while opposing actions produce canceling effects.
During multiple-goal pursuit, people depart from mathematical optimality in risk-predictable ways: risk-averse prioritization for approach goals and risk-seeking prioritization for avoidance goals.
Disease threat cues, whether chronic traits or situationally primed, increase categorization of neutral targets as outgroup members, an effect amplified for racial outgroups.
A request preceded by a rejected extreme request generates more compliance than an identical request presented alone; a reciprocal concessions norm mediates the effect.
Moral considerations bias folk judgments of intentional action; morally negative actions are judged as intentional more readily than structurally identical morally neutral actions.

Logically equivalent comparative statements framed as 'more than' versus 'less than' produce asymmetric responses; 'more than' statements are preferred and judged as true more readily due to cognitive fluency differences.

People hold stereotypically negative views of profit-seeking firms and neglect the societal benefits of profit incentives; prompting consideration of long-run profit logic attenuates anti-profit beliefs.

Truthful statements used to mislead (paltering) is a common negotiation strategy distinct from omission and commission lying; negotiators palter more than lie by commission, but paltering increases impasse likelihood and reputation harm.
Awe experiences promote humility through self-diminishment; awe-prone individuals display greater humility and more balanced self-assessments of strengths and weaknesses.
